Class IrrelevantTermLabelsProperty

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.equality.IrrelevantTermLabelsProperty
All Implemented Interfaces:
Property<JTerm>
public class IrrelevantTermLabelsProperty extends Object implements Property<JTerm>
A property that can be used in EqualsModProperty.equalsModProperty(Object, Property, Object[]) for terms. All irrelevant term labels are ignored in this equality check.

The single instance of this property can be accessed through IRRELEVANT_TERM_LABELS_PROPERTY.

    <V> boolean
    equalsModThisProperty(JTerm term1, JTerm term2, V... v)
    Checks if term2 is a term syntactically equal to term1, except for some irrelevant labels.
    int
    hashCodeModThisProperty(JTerm term)
    Computes the hash code of term while ignoring irrelevant labels.

  • Method Details

    • equalsModThisProperty

      public <V> boolean equalsModThisProperty(JTerm term1, JTerm term2, V... v)
      Checks if term2 is a term syntactically equal to term1, except for some irrelevant labels.
      Specified by:
      equalsModThisProperty in interface Property<JTerm>
      Type Parameters:
      V - is not needed for this equality check
      Parameters:
      term1 - a term
      term2 - the term compared to term1
      v - should not be used for this equality check
      Returns:
      true iff term2 is a term syntactically equal to term1, except for their irrelevant labels.
      See Also:
      • isStrategyRelevant

    • hashCodeModThisProperty

      public int hashCodeModThisProperty(JTerm term)
      Computes the hash code of term while ignoring irrelevant labels.
      Specified by:
      hashCodeModThisProperty in interface Property<JTerm>
      Parameters:
      term - the term to compute the hash code for
      Returns:
      the hash code
